tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, low total CO₂, significant proteinuria, ketonuria, and glucosuria, with low urine specific gravity and abnormal urine appearance. These findings suggest impaired renal function, possible early diabetic nephropathy or other renal pathology, and metabolic derangement. HbA1c is unexpectedly low, possibly due to anemia or recent changes in glycemic control. Recommend further renal evaluation and correlation with clinical findings.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Acute ST segment elevation myocardial infarction
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
